identity.title: Bandgap Reference Macro Model
identity.supported_scope: pure voltage-domain event-driven transient behavioral Verilog-A

parameters.name: tr
parameters.override_behavior: sets output and metric transition smoothing

parameters.name: vth
parameters.override_behavior: sets the voltage-coded clk and rst threshold

parameters.name: vstart
parameters.override_behavior: sets the supply level required for startup and valid updates

parameters.name: vref
parameters.override_behavior: sets the nominal regulated reference target before line correction and clamping

properties.observable_contract: Reset or vin below vstart forces out and metric to 0 V.
On eligible rising clock crossings, the held reference advances by 0.35 of the remaining error to the clamped line-corrected target.
The line-corrected target is clamped to 0 through vin minus 0.05 V, and driven out remains within 0 through 0.9 V.
Metric is 0 V in reset or brownout, 0.2 V during startup below the 0.48 V validity threshold, and 0.9 V after the held reference exceeds it.
Above startup, the reference state changes only on rising clock crossings and holds between samples.

modeling_constraints: Use deterministic voltage-domain sampled settling with no KCL or KVL regulation loop.
Do not use current contributions, transistor-level devices, AC/noise analysis, or validation side channels.
